title: Metrics Upsert API Endpoint for Third-Party Integrations
description:
## Context

External integrations need the ability to programmatically create and update metrics in DataTools Pro. A new API endpoint was needed to accept metric data as JSON and perform an upsert (insert or update) operation.

## Endpoint

`POST /api/v1_0/metrics/upsert` — accepts a JSON array of metric objects and creates or updates them for the authenticated team.

### Request Payload
The endpoint accepts an array of metric objects, each containing:
- `id` (optional) — public-facing metric ID for updates; omit for new metrics
- `name` (required, string) — metric name
- `definition` (optional, string) — metric definition
- `business_owner` (optional, string) — business owner name
- `target_interval` (optional, string)
- `primary_lead` (optional)
- `dimensionality` (optional)
- `description` (optional, string) — metric description
- `logic` (optional, string) — calculation logic
- `aliases` (optional, array) — each with `alias` (required string) and `event_type` ("update" or "delete")

### Authentication
Requires a valid API token created from `/user/tokens/`. Coda tokens are explicitly rejected with an "Unauthorized or invalid token type" error.

### Error Handling
- Invalid JSON format: "Invalid JSON format. Expected an array of metrics."
- Wrong token type (Coda token): "Unauthorized or invalid token type."
- Non-existent metric ID on update: "Metric with the provided ID {id} not found."
- Validation errors returned per field (name required, types enforced)
- Unexpected errors caught with try/catch returning the error message
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1_0/metrics/upsert accepts a JSON array of metric objects
Metrics without an ID are created as new metrics for the authenticated team
Metrics with a valid public ID are updated in place
Requesting an update with a non-existent metric ID returns a clear error message
Coda tokens are rejected with an unauthorized error
Invalid JSON payloads return a descriptive format error
Field validation errors are returned per-field (name required, correct types)
Alias operations support create, update, and delete via event_type
Metrics are scoped to the authenticated user's team
Unexpected errors are caught and returned as JSON with error details
middleware_changes: New upsert controller action with validation rules for each metric field, token type verification (API tokens only, Coda tokens rejected), and comprehensive error handling with try/catch wrapping.
data_model_changes: Metrics can now be created and updated via API. Alias records support event_type-based operations (update/delete) through the upsert flow.
api_changes: POST /api/v1_0/metrics/upsert — new endpoint accepting a JSON array of metric objects. Supports batch create/update with per-field validation. Returns created/updated metric objects on success or structured error responses on failure.
implementation_notes: Tokens currently have a one-to-one relationship with users rather than teams. The team_id is derived from the token owner. Waqar raised the question of whether tokens should be directly associated with teams instead. The endpoint explicitly checks token type and rejects Coda tokens to prevent cross-integration auth issues. Postman collection was shared for testing.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented the upsert endpoint on staging with full validation and error handling, shared the Postman collection for testing, and raised a design question about whether API tokens should be associated with teams directly rather than through the user relationship.
